full_prompt: Design a monolithic 6061-T6 aluminum front bulkhead plate with integrated roll-hoop attachment bosses for a collegiate fuel-efficiency Combustion Prototype benchmark vehicle using a spec 200 cc OHV single-cylinder engine (5.5 hp governed, produces peak torque ~9 N-m at countershaft). The bulkhead is a single billet-machined 6061-T6 part (no welds, no bolted gussets, integral bosses). Rule context: the benchmark requires a driver minimum 59 kg (+ballast if lighter), the course is 2.6 km at 15-25 mph average. The bulkhead separates the driver compartment from the engine bay, faces forward, and anchors the front roll-hoop and the front suspension pickup points. Geometry: 500 mm wide x 350 mm tall x 6 mm thick plate, with an integral 20 mm tall perimeter flange on the rear face (5 mm wall) forming a rigid box section. Four integral bosses for roll-hoop attachment: each boss is a cylinder 30 mm diameter x 25 mm long with a 12 mm (M12 clearance) through-bore, placed at the four corners of a 400 mm wide x 300 mm tall rectangle on the front face. Two integral front-suspension pickup bosses: each boss 20 mm diameter x 20 mm long with a 10 mm bore at 200 mm lateral from the vertical centerline and 100 mm above the plate bottom edge. Central 80 mm diameter hole for steering column pass-through. Overall bounding envelope 500 x 350 x 35 mm. Material 6061-T6 with E 68.9 GPa, Poisson's ratio 0.33, density 2700 kg/m^3, yield 276 MPa, UTS 310 MPa. Three load cases. Load Case 1 (roll-hoop side load): 3.5 kN lateral at any one of the top roll-hoop bosses representing a partial rollover 1.5x driver weight (59 kg driver + 30 kg vehicle structure = 89 kg * 1.5g * 0.5 top-fraction ~ 650 N, but the benchmark rule inflates to 3.5 kN per AISI D (benchmark Rulebook Section 3.7 roll-hoop) acting parallel to the ground; the three other bosses are fully fixed. Load Case 2 (front impact): 2.5 kN longitudinal rearward applied distributed across the front face from a low-speed frontal impact (15 mph into a barrier), all four roll-hoop bosses held fixed at the rear-facing corners. Load Case 3 (suspension pickup bump): 1.8 kN vertical up-load at each of the two front-suspension bosses (2.0g bump on 45 kg front corner weight = 880 N plus dynamic 2x = 1760 N rounded to 1800 N), roll-hoop bosses fully fixed. Pass/fail: (R1) under LC1 roll-hoop side load, peak von Mises must not exceed 138 MPa (yield/2.0 benchmark R3.7 structural allowable); (R2) under LC2 front impact, peak stress must not exceed 184 MPa (yield/1.5 transient); (R3) under LC3 bump, peak stress 138 MPa; (R4) maximum deflection at any roll-hoop boss under LC1 must not exceed 5 mm to preserve roll-hoop geometric integrity; (R5) first six natural frequencies must exceed 40 Hz to avoid coupling with the 40-60 Hz single-cylinder engine power-stroke harmonic at 3200 rpm governed; (R6) bulkhead mass must not exceed 1.8 kg per the benchmark lightweight design objective (less is better but 1.8 is a hard ceiling for the competitive field). Thermal, fluid, buckling, and radiation analyses are not required. Deliver STEP AP242 geometry plus report.
short_prompt:
Design a monolithic 6061-T6 aluminum front bulkhead plate with integrated roll-hoop attachment bosses for a collegiate fuel-efficiency Combustion Prototype vehicle (spec 200 cc OHV single, ~5.5 hp, 9 N-m peak countershaft torque, 59 kg minimum driver). The bulkhead is a single billet-machined part with a rear perimeter box-flange, four corner roll-hoop bosses, two front-suspension pickup bosses, and a central steering-column pass-through. Design per the benchmark Rulebook Section 3.7 roll-hoop requirement, using yield/2.0 continuous and yield/1.5 transient safety factors.

Envelope: 500 x 350 x 6 mm plate (overall ~500 x 350 x 35 mm with flange and bosses).

Load cases.
- LC1 roll-hoop side load: 3.5 kN lateral at any one top roll-hoop boss (benchmark R3.7 inflated-rollover demonstration), other three bosses fixed.
- LC2 front impact: 2.5 kN longitudinal rearward distributed on the front face (15 mph barrier), all four roll-hoop corner bosses fixed at the rear face.
- LC3 suspension bump: 1.8 kN vertical up at each front-suspension boss (2 g bump on front axle), roll-hoop bosses fixed.
Also check first six natural frequencies above 40 Hz to avoid the 40-60 Hz power-stroke band at 3200 rpm.

prompt.title: Collegiate fuel-efficiency benchmark front bulkhead w/ roll-hoop attachments
prompt.brief: Billet 6061-T6 plate 500x350x6 with integral perimeter flange + 4 roll-hoop bosses + 2 front-suspension bosses + central steering column hole.

exclusions_explained.thermal_analysis: Separate from engine bay thermal; engine isolation is outside bulkhead scope.
exclusions_explained.fluid_analysis: Aero loads not dominant for bulkhead.
exclusions_explained.buckling_analysis: Plate thickness makes buckling non-critical at these loads.
